title: Streaming Analysis, Chunk Boundaries, and Resource Budgets · Language Clinic: Batch Stream Equivalence
summary: A large-file mode still calls read_text, splits tokens at chunk edges, decodes partial UTF-8 incorrectly, retains every token, and claims bounded memory without measurement. Predict the user decision, input identity, text policy, expected report, failure boundary, and evidence that could disprove the proposed implementation before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the batch stream equivalence increment in bounded streaming analyzer. Learners progress through predict…
objectives: Compare batch and streaming results across adversarial split points, malformed data, and final fragments.
Set byte, vocabulary, token-fragment, line, output, and elapsed-time budgets with rejection behavior.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Evidence: bookbot-worked-bookbot-streaming-bounded-memory-3
# Competency: bookbot-worked-bookbot-streaming-bounded-memory-3.
# Case 5798: a large-file migration rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: retain source permission, digest purpose, revision, Python, Unicode, policy, command, and output identity.
# Changed case: split one word and one UTF-8 sequence across stream chunks and compare batch output byte-for-byte.
def worked_bookbot_streaming_bounded_memory_3(chunks: int = 8, batch_tokens: int = 120, stream_tokens: int = 120, peak_bytes: int = 8192, memory_budget: int = 16384, fragments_closed: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked3 = "worked3-3dekrow-5798"
    assert evidence_variant_worked3
    evidence_axes_worked3 = (
        "corpusworked3",
        "policy3dekrow",
        "revision5798",
        "commandworked35798",
        "report3dekrow5798",
        "test5798worked3",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked3)) == 6
    if chunks < 1 or batch_tokens < 0 or stream_tokens < 0:
        return False, stream_tokens, "stream-shape"
    if stream_tokens != batch_tokens:
        return False, stream_tokens, "batch-stream-drift"
    if peak_bytes < 0 or peak_bytes > memory_budget:
        return False, peak_bytes, "memory-budget"
    if not fragments_closed:
        return False, chunks, "partial-token"
    assert peak_bytes <= memory_budget
    return True, stream_tokens, "bounded-stream"

content.code:
# Evidence: bookbot-batch-stream-equivalence-1k43re
# Competency: bookbot-batch-stream-equivalence.
# Case 3097: an assistive-technology terminal review.
# Operating constraint: retain source permission, digest purpose, revision, Python, Unicode, policy, command, and output identity.
# Changed case: replace an ASCII word with a canonically equivalent combining sequence and compare the declared counts.
def evidence_bookbot_batch_stream_equivalence_1k43re(chunks: int = 8, batch_tokens: int = 120, stream_tokens: int = 120, peak_bytes: int = 8192, memory_budget: int = 16384, fragments_closed: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1k43re = "1k43re-er34k1-3097"
    assert evidence_variant_1k43re
    evidence_axes_1k43re = (
        "corpus1k43re",
        "policyer34k1",
        "revision3097",
        "command1k43re3097",
        "reporter34k13097",
        "test30971k43re",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1k43re)) == 6
    if chunks < 1 or batch_tokens < 0 or stream_tokens < 0:
        return False, stream_tokens, "stream-shape"
    if stream_tokens != batch_tokens:
        return False, stream_tokens, "batch-stream-drift"
    if peak_bytes < 0 or peak_bytes > memory_budget:
        return False, peak_bytes, "memory-budget"
    if not fragments_closed:
        return False, chunks, "partial-token"
    assert peak_bytes <= memory_budget
    return True, stream_tokens, "bounded-stream"

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Compare batch and streaming results across adversarial split points, malformed data, and final fragments.

hints: For Bookbot and Python Defend the Batch Stream Equivalence model · Theory Batch Stream Equivalence, name the stakeholder, corpus authority, revision, path, bytes, encoding, Unicode, token, count, rank, report, command, test, package, release, and transfer boundary, Batch Stream Equivalence precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Bookbot and Python Defend the Batch Stream Equivalence model · Theory Batch Stream Equivalence, trace this concrete input and predict admission, decoding, normalization, tokenization, counting, ranking, formatting, stdout, stderr, status, test, package, failure, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A theory Bookbot team handles case 6343 during an assistive-technology terminal review. Build deterministic Python evidence to prove streaming and batch equivalence under token-fragment and peak-memory bounds; record the first failed boundary, causal evidence, repair, regression test, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner; then install the built wheel in a fresh environment and rerun success, invalid-argument, and decode-failure cases. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Compare batch and streaming results across adversarial split points, malformed data, and final fragments.

For Bookbot and Python Defend the Batch Stream Equivalence model · Theory Batch Stream Equivalence, challenge the documented misconception with changed-file, changed-encoding, Unicode, token, ranking, stream, CLI, test, package, security, accessibility, failure, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: One ordinary file is sufficient evidence that streaming matches batch analysis.

checks.description: The cumulative python portfolio contains this competency’s named evidence increment.
checks.failureMessage: Add the exact evidence marker # Evidence: bookbot-batch-stream-equivalence-1k43re.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: bookbot-batch-stream-equivalence-1k43re(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_bookbot_batch_stream_equivalence_1k43re\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?chunks < 1)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?stream_tokens != batch_tokens)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?peak_bytes > memory_budget)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?not fragments_closed)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
